chunk_summary: Guidelines for feeding children aged 12–59 months with moderate acute malnutrition (MAM), focusing on meal frequency, energy density, protein sources, and caregiver-specific counselling for sustainable home diet therapy.
learning_objectives: Understand feeding prescriptions for children aged 12–59 months with moderate acute malnutrition (MAM).
Learn to counsel caregivers on meal frequency, energy density, and protein sources for children with MAM.
Identify practical strategies to integrate supplementary rations without displacing home foods.
Document and monitor feeding plans effectively for follow-up visits.
key_facts: For ages 12–23 months: prescribe 3–4 meals and 1–2 snacks daily, with portions of three-quarters to one 250 mL cup per meal.
For ages 24–59 months: prescribe 3 main meals and 2 snacks daily, avoiding low-nutrient grazing like tea and biscuits.
Energy density can be increased by adding oil or ghee to meals and using protein anchors like eggs, dal, or legumes.
Diluted milk reduces calorie and protein density; use full-strength milk without replacing solids.
Counselling should include a documented plan with a problem statement, agreed changes, and a monitoring question for follow-up.
reference_quote: Guideline on the prevention and management of wasting and nutritional oedema in infants and children under 5 years — World Health Organization, 2023.
document_key_topics: Feeding prescriptions for children with MAM
Counselling caregivers on home diet therapy
Energy density and protein sources in child nutrition
